title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Yemen's Iranian-backed Houthis say they will block Saudi shipping at Red Sea gateway
stories.summary:
Yemen's Iranian-backed Houthis say they will block Saudi shipping at Red Sea gateway. Yemen's Iranian-backed Houthi rebels on Monday announced they are imposing a maritime embargo against Saudi Arabia in retaliation for a blockade on Yemen and a recent attack on Sanaa International Airport, threatening another vital waterway for global shipping and raising fears of renewed conflict.

Al Jazeera: Yemen’s Houthis declare naval blockade of Saudi Arabia: What to know. Al Jazeera reports that yemeni group says the announcement is based on the principle of 'an eye for an eye'.

Sinaloa cartel cofounder Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada was sentenced on Monday to a mandatory life sentence in federal prison after admitting he spent more than five decades helping build one of the world’s largest drug trafficking organizations in Mexico. stories.title: The Parasite Blamed On Taco Bell Lettuce? Not So Fast
stories.summary:
The Parasite Blamed On Taco Bell Lettuce? Not So Fast.

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) said on Sunday that it wrongly flagged lettuce from Taco Bell as a potential source of the outbreak of the “explosive diarrhea” parasite across the country.

In the midst of a nationwide eruption of cyclosporiasis, the FDA investigated Taco Bell’s lettuce supplier Taylor Farms for a potential contamination ... BBC: Test that found source of US explosive diarrhoea outbreak was false positive, health officials say.

BBC reports that the latest update from the US Food and Drug Administration means the exact source of the parasite is still not known.

stories.summary:
J.D. and Usha Vance welcome a baby boy, the first child born to a sitting VP in more than 150 years. Vice President J.D.

Vance and his wife, second lady Usha Vance, on Sunday announced the birth of their fourth child, the first born to a sitting vice president in more than 150 years.

BBC: JD Vance says wife Usha and son 'happy and healthy' after birth of fourth child. BBC reports that the couple welcomed their fourth child, Alec Neel Vance, on Sunday morning.

stories.title: Armed Man Triggers Terrorism Investigation Outside ICE Office
stories.summary:
Armed Man Triggers Terrorism Investigation Outside ICE Office. A man believed to be an anti-ICE activist armed with two airsoft rifles deployed an incendiary device outside 26 Federal Plaza in New York City on Monday morning, prompting a swift response from federal and local law enforcement.

At least two people were injured in the incident after the suspect “menaced” bystanders with the airsoft ... Fox News: FBI alleges suspect 'deployed an incendiary device outside' federal building.

Fox News reports that the person who placed 'an incendiary device outside' a federal building in New York City on Monday is in custody, FBI Director Kash Patel noted.

stories.title: Telehealth ban still standing between dying patients and their doctors
stories.summary: Telehealth ban still standing between dying patients and their doctors. stories.title: Inside the horrifying online network where children extort others to commit violence and self-harm
stories.summary:
Inside the horrifying online network where children extort others to commit violence and self-harm. The 764 network is just one part of a growing online ecosystem that pushes young members into brutal and degrading acts.

It has perpetrators and victims in dozens of countries In late 2024, in a quiet suburb of Stockholm, a 14-year-old boy approached a man in his 80s who was using a walker.

He stabbed the man three times in the back. The video of the attack in Hässelby was streamed and spread quickly.

It fed a global online ecosystem that actively encourages and glorifies acts of.

stories.summary:
Experts warn of rise in sadistic online exploitation of vulnerable children. Global increase in cases of predators coercing children to harm themselves or produce child sexual abuse material Cases of predators coercing children into producing sexual photos and videos of themselves have surged globally in the past year, a cruel phenomenon known as “sadistic online exploitation”, child safety experts and law enforcement officials warn.

In cases of sadistic online exploitation, perpetrators target vulnerable teens on social media platforms and online games. T.

stories.title: Apple Crumble: a tasty Knives Out-inspired mystery that lets you snoop on your own family
stories.summary:
Apple Crumble: a tasty Knives Out-inspired mystery that lets you snoop on your own family. The developer of Duck Detective reveals details of her ‘sad grandma game’ follow-up: a whodunnit – or who-plans-to-do-it? – with a darker edge Someone wants to kill grandma.

In this Knives Out - inspired mystery game, each of your family members is suspicious in their own way: your stressed mum, your “midlife crisis uncle” and especially that stranger in your bedroom. Yikes!

stories.title: Duterte impeachment trial: Senate junks bid vs AMLC report
stories.summary:
Duterte impeachment trial: Senate junks bid vs AMLC report. MANILA, Philippines — The Senate Impeachment Court has junked the defense panel’s opposition to the prosecution’s request for a subpoena of the Anti-Money Laundering Council (AMLC) report on Vice President Sara Duterte’s financial transactions, saying precedent allows courts and Congress to access such records.

Toward the end of Monday’s trial, Presiding Officer and Senator-Judge Francis. Rappler: LIST: Companies covered by VP Sara Duterte records subpoena.

Rappler reports that the Senate impeachment court's subpoena covers at least 20 companies linked to the Vice President and her husband, Mans Carpio. Meanwhile, the subpoena request for the financial records of two other companies is denied.

stories.title: WATCH: Chel Diokno welcomes granting subpoena for Duterte records
stories.summary:
WATCH: Chel Diokno welcomes granting subpoena for Duterte records. MANILA, Philippines – House prosecutor and Akbayan Rep. Chel Diokno joins Inquirer.net Neil Mercado in an interview following the impeachment court’s ruling that granted the prosecution’s request to subpoena the bank, tax, and Anti-Money Laundering Council (AMLC) records of Vice President Sara Duterte and her husband Manases Carpio.

The subpoena forms part of the proceedings. Philippine Daily Inquirer: Defense team on granting of subpoena: Expected, not a huge setback.

Philippine Daily Inquirer reports that mANILA, Philippines — Vice President Sara Duterte’s defense team on Monday said it “anticipated” the Senate impeachment court’s decision to issue subpoenas for their client’s financial and tax records, even saying that they do not count it as a major setback.

Defense spokesperson Michael Poa made the pronouncement f

stories.title: NTF-Elcac committed to protecting IP kids from Reds’ influence
stories.summary:
NTF-Elcac committed to protecting IP kids from Reds’ influence.

MANILA, Philippines – A ranking official of the National Task Force to End Local Communist Armed Conflict (NTF-Elcac) on Monday reiterated the government’s commitment to protecting Indigenous Peoples (IP) children from recruitment and influence of communist insurgents.

NTF-Elcac executive director Undersecretary Ernesto Torres Jr. made the comment on the second anniversary of the landmark judgment rendered.

stories.title: Gov’t expands K2K program to strengthen reintegration of returning OFWs
stories.summary:
Gov’t expands K2K program to strengthen reintegration of returning OFWs.

MANILA, Philippines — The government on Monday expanded the Kaalaman Tungo sa Kabuhayan (K2K) program to provide returning overseas Filipino workers (OFWs) with more opportunities for skills training, employment, and livelihood as they reintegrate into their communities.

The Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE) led the signing of a joint memorandum circular institutionalizing the expanded.

stories.title: Reject broken systems – DOJ exec to future lawyers, public servants
stories.summary:
Reject broken systems – DOJ exec to future lawyers, public servants.

MANILA, Philippines — Despite widespread corruption allegations involving flood control projects, Justice Undersecretary Margarita Gutierrez challenged future lawyers and public servants to reject broken systems, even if they had apparently been accepted as normal, and remember that public office is a responsibility entrusted by the people.

Gutierrez made her remark during her speech on July.
